mcqs.question: Can an abstract class be instantiated directly?
mcqs.explanation: Abstract classes cannot be instantiated directly because they are designed to be inherited by other classes.

mcqs.question: What is a characteristic of an abstract method?

options.text: It has no body and ends with a semicolon

mcqs.explanation: An abstract method has no body and must be overridden by any non-abstract subclass.

mcqs.question: What must be true of a class that contains an abstract method?
mcqs.explanation: Any class that contains an abstract method must itself be declared as an abstract class.

mcqs.question: What is a requirement for a concrete subclass of an abstract class?

options.text: It must implement all abstract methods
It must only implement some abstract methods
It need not override any methods

mcqs.explanation: A concrete subclass of an abstract class must provide an implementation for all abstract methods.

mcqs.question: Can an abstract class have a constructor?
mcqs.explanation: Abstract classes can have constructors, which are called by the constructors of their subclasses.

mcqs.question: When should you use an abstract class?

options.text: When you want to provide a common base with shared implementation
When you want to define a contract that must be implemented
When you want to create a utility class
When you want to create a singleton class

mcqs.explanation: Abstract classes are useful when you want to provide a common base with shared implementation for a group of related classes.

mcqs.question: What is a key difference between abstract classes and interfaces?

options.text: Abstract classes can have constructors, while interfaces cannot
Abstract classes can have abstract methods, while interfaces cannot
Abstract classes can have state, while interfaces cannot
Abstract classes are single inheritance, while interfaces are multiple inheritance

mcqs.explanation: Abstract classes are used for single inheritance, while interfaces are used for multiple inheritance.
